problem: At the grocery store, I bought $4$ different items. I brought $3$ identical bags, and handed them to the cashier. How many ways are there for the cashier to put the items I bought in the $3$ identical bags, assuming he might leave some of the bags empty?

classified_questions.question: What are the different cases we can consider when distributing the four items into the three identical bags?
classified_questions.label: r

classified_questions.question: For the first case where all four items go in the same bag, how many ways are there to do this?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: How many ways can we choose one item to be in a bag by itself while the other three items go in another bag?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: In the case where two items go in one bag and the other two go in another bag, how many ways can we choose which items go together?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: Since the bags are identical, do we need to consider the order in which the items are placed in the bags?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: What is the total number of ways when two items go in one bag and the other two items each go in a different bag?
classified_questions.label: b
